titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/237

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=566 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:04:28.302411+00 | translation moa=567 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:04:29.739244+00

div.p:
Alex’ dei Gratia Rex scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus Totius terre sue salutem . Noueritis nos inspexisse scriptum conuencionum factarum apud Berewyc in nostra presencia inter . Pa
tricium Comitem de Dunbar’ nepotem nostrum ex parte vna et abbatem et conuentum de Melros ex altera super quadam terra in territorio de halsington’ quam Magister Willelmus de
Grenlawe dedit in plena Curia dicti Comitis de Dunbar predictis Abbati et conuentui tamquam suis assignatis in hec uerba confectum . Omnibus Christi fidelibus hoc scriptum
uisuris uel audituris . Patricius Comes de Dunbar Salutem eternam in domino . Sciant omnes tam presentes quam futuri quod cum Magister . Willelmus . de Grenlawe quandam terram
quam habuit ex dono Roberti de Muscamp’ tenentis nostri in feodo de halsington’ sibi et suis assignatis donatam  Abbati et conuentui de Melros tamquam suis assig
natis dedisset sollempniter in Curia nostra de Eddirham’ . et idem Magister . Willelmus de eadem terra ibidem se dissaisisset et ipsos sollempniter saississet in eandem  vt
ipsorum abbatis et conuentus possessioni pacifice et perpetue futuris temporibus prouideretur  Ita inter ipsos ex parte vna et nos ex altera in presencia karissimi domini nostri . Alexandri . illustris
Regis scott’ et baronum suorum conuenit . quod nos promisimus concessimus et hac presenti carta nostra nos et heredes nostros obligauimus quod dictum tenementum ipsis Abbati
et conuentui contra omnes homines adquietabimus . warantizabimus et inperpetuum defendemus . Tenend’ et habend’ eisdem Abbati et conuentui in liberam et perpe
tuam elemosinam per illas diuisas per quas dictus Magister Willelmus eam tenuit et cum omnibus pertinentiis libertatibus et aisiamentis ad eam pertinentibus et cum comuni
pastura ville de halsington’ . et cum libero introitu et exitu ad eandem pasturam et ad dicta aisiamenta . Adeo integre . libere . quiete et honorifice ab omni
seculari seruicio exaccione et demanda  sicut aliqua elemosina in Regno Scocie . liberius quiecius . plenius et honorificencius tenetur aut possidetur . Et si forte
aliquis contra dictos Abbatem et conuentum de Melros . super premissis mouerit calumpniam  et nos uel heredes nostri eos non defenderimus  Ita quod oporteat ipsos
dictam terram amittere  aut de ipsa per iudicium eici  Nos uel heredes nostri dabimus ipsi calumpniatori rationabilem extentam in dominicis nostris uel alibi in loco compe
tenti ad ualorem dicte terre . et ita in omnibus eidem satisfaciemus  quod dicti Abbas et conuentus de Melros dictam terram quam Magister Willelmus de Grenlawe eis .
contulit liberam et quietam pacifice et continue in perpetuum retinebunt ac possidebunt . Nos autem ad omnia predicta facienda pro nobis et heredibus nostris 
corporale prestitimus iuramentum . et nosmet ipsos et heredes nostros plenius obligauimus . Subicientes nos et heredes nostros iurisdiccioni Episcopi Glasguensis
qui pro tempore fuerit  vt preter potestatem regiam per quam debemus et uolumus coherceri  dictus . episcopus . nos et heredes nostros ad omnium predictorum obseruanciam
sublato omni iuris remedio et appellacione cessante per censuram ecclesiasticam ualeat compellere . Et ut hoc scriptum Robur firmitatis optineat 
illud sigilli nostri apposicione fecimus roborari  Actum apud Berewyc . decimo quarto . die Aprilis . Anno Regni domini Regis Alexandri . Tricesimo quarto . Testibus .
Magistro . Gamelino . Dominis Roberto de Ros’ . Dauid’ de Graham’ . Roberto de Meiners . Willelmo filio Comitis . Thoma de Normanuill’ . Johanne Cumyn . Hen
rico de Graham’ . Nicholao Corbeth’ . Waltero filio Walteri senescalli . Roberto filio Comitis . Thoma de boscho . Bernardo de Ryppell’ et multis aliis . Nos
igitur ad eorumdem Patricii Comitis . et abbatis et conuentus instanciam conuenciones predictas concessimus et eas presenti carta nostra et
autoritate regia confirmauimus . Testibus . Willelmo Comite de Marr’ . Alexandro senescallo . Roberto de Ros . Willelmo de Lyndes’ . Willelmo Olifard’ . Nicholao
de Sules . Willelmo capellano nostro . Alexandro clerico de Capella . Thoma clerico de liberacione et aliis apud Berewyc . vicesimo die Aprilis Anno Regni domini
Regis Tricesimo quarto

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, greeting. Know us to have inspected the writing of agreements made at Berwick in our presence between Patrick, earl of Dunbar, our nephew, on the one side, and the abbot and convent of Melrose on the other, about a land in the territory of Hassington which Master William of Greenlaw gave in the full court of the said Earl of Dunbar to the said abbot and convent as well as to their assigns, drawn up in these words: To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear this writing, Patrick, earl of Dunbar, sends greeting. Let everyone present and future know that, since Master William of Greenlaw had solemnly given in our court at Edrom, to the abbot and convent of Melrose as well as to their assigns, a certain land that he had from the gift of Robert de Muschamp our tenant in the feu of Hassington, donated to him and his heirs and assigns, and since the same Master William had put himself out of possession of the same land in that place and solemnly put them in possession of the same, so that the possession of the abbot and convent is peaceably and perpetually established for future times, between them on the one side and us on the other, in the presence of our well-beloved lord Alexander, illustrious king of the Scots, and his barons; it was agreed that we promise, grant, and by this our present charter bind ourselves and our heirs, that we would acquit, warrant, and defend for ever the said holding to the abbot and convent against all people. For the same abbot and convent to hold and to have in free and perpetual alms, by the boundaries by which the said Master William held it and with all pertinents, liberties, and easements pertaining to it, and with common pasture of the toun of Hassington, and with free entry and exit to the same pasture and to the said easments, as wholly, freely, quitly, and honourably from every secular service, exaction, and demand, just as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is most freely, quitly, fully, and honourably held and possessed. And if anyone move a claim against the said Abbot and convent of Melrose concerning the foregoing, and we or our heirs do not defended them, so that it becomes necessary for them to lose the said land, or be ejected from it by judgment; we or our heirs shall give the reasonable value to the claimant in our demesne or elsewhere in a suitable place to the value of the said land; and so we shall make satisfaction to the same in everything, that the said abbot and convent of Melrose shall retain and possess the said land which Master William of Greenlaw conveyed to them free and quit, peaceably and continuously forever. We, however, in order to do the aforesaid things, for the sake of ourselves and our heirs have provided a bodily oath, and we have the more fully obliged ourselves and our heirs, submitting ourselves and our heirs to the jurisdiction of the Bishop of Glasgow -- whoever it shall be at the time -- so that besides the royal power, by which we ought and wish to be constrained, the said bishop can compel us and our heirs by ecclesiastical censure, with every legal remedy removed and without appeal, to the observance of all the aforesaid things. And so that this writing retain authority and strength, we have caused it to be authenticated by the application of our seal. Enacted at Berwick on the fourteenth day of April in the thirty-fourth year of the reign of the lord king, Alexander. As witnesses, Master Gamelin, the lords Robert of Roos, David of Graham, Robert of Menzies, William fitz Count, Thomas de Normanville, John Comyn, Henry of Graham, Nicholas Corbet, Walter son of Walter the steward, Robert fitz Count, Thomas del Bois, Bernard of Ripley, and many others. At the request of the same Earl Patrick, the abbot and convent, we have therefore licensed the aforesaid agreements and made them firm by our present charter and royal authority. As witnesses, William earl of Mar, Alexander the steward, Robert of Roos, William of Lindsay, William Oliphant, Nicholas de Soulis, William our chaplain, Alexander the clerk of the chapel, Thomas the clerk of the livery, and others. At Berwick on the twentieth day of April in the thirty-fourth year of the reign of the lord king.
